themes: Long wait times dominate the backlog — the majority of contacts have been waiting over 100 days, with many exceeding 200–350 days, signaling an urgent need for a faster, more scalable outreach and follow-up process.

Multi-contact accounts (Travel Leaders Group, Berkshire Hathaway HomeServices, Holiday Inn Express, Forty Winks, and others) appear repeatedly, suggesting these customers have broad or unresolved needs that may benefit from a consolidated account-level conversation rather than individual touchpoints.

The backlog skews almost entirely toward Managers, pointing to frontline workflow pain — concerns are likely centered on day-to-day usability, reporting efficiency, and time savings at the location level rather than strategic or administrative issues.
